name: Unit Banking-as-a-Service API
what_it_does: Full-stack Banking-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform with REST API — enabling fintech companies and software platforms to embed bank accounts, debit cards, ACH payments, wire transfers, and lending into their products without becoming a bank, with comprehensive developer API.
use_cases: Creating FDIC-insured bank accounts for customers via API (checking, savings)
Issuing physical and virtual debit cards to users programmatically
Initiating ACH, wire, and check payments on behalf of account holders
Querying transaction data and account balances for embedded finance dashboards
Building credit/lending products using Unit's lending API extension
not_for: Consumer banking apps (Unit is B2B for businesses embedding finance)
International banking outside the US (Unit is US-only BaaS)
best_when: A B2B SaaS company, marketplace, or fintech wants to embed FDIC-insured banking (accounts, cards, payments) into their product for US customers without a banking license.
avoid_when: You need international banking, serve consumers directly, or want crypto/digital asset features.

auth.notes: Bearer token from Unit dashboard. Separate tokens for sandbox and production. Scoped access for customer management, payments, and admin. Customer tokens for end-user actions. Application-level and customer-level token distinction.

pricing.paid_tiers: Revenue share + monthly platform fee; contact sales
pricing.notes: Revenue share model for production. Free sandbox for development. US-based (San Francisco). Israeli founders. Backed by Accel, Better Tomorrow Ventures. Banking partner: Blue Ridge Bank (FDIC insured). Strong developer experience. Used by 500+ fintech companies. YC backed.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per contract; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent developer documentation — one of the best in BaaS. REST API with comprehensive coverage of accounts, customers, cards, payments, transactions. Separate sandbox/production environments. Webhook for all account events. Customer vs application token distinction critical. KYC/KYB flows require customer onboarding before account creation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported for payment operations. Account and customer creation idempotent by reference. Transaction queries idempotent. Card operations include idempotency support.
known_agent_gotchas: KYC/KYB customer verification required before account creation — async process
Application token vs customer token — different permissions and use cases
Sandbox and production have different API endpoints — always verify environment
Banking regulations affect what operations agents can automate without human review

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and PCI-DSS certified. US data residency. Bearer tokens with application vs customer scope separation. Sandbox isolation. FDIC-insured through Blue Ridge Bank. Banking-grade security. KYC/AML compliance built-in. Encryption at rest and in transit.
